# @zakkster/lite-headless / radio-group

ARIA `role="radiogroup"` + `role="radio"` per item. Single-select,
exclusive, form-semantic. The right primitive for **Twitch Prediction
outcomes**, **Poll options**, anything with "pick one and commit".

## Distinct from toggle-group

| | radio-group | toggle-group |
|---|---|---|
| ARIA role | `radiogroup` + `radio` | `group` + `aria-pressed` |
| Selection | exactly one | one or many |
| Semantic | form field | UI mode toggle |
| Use for | poll options, settings | text-align, view modes, filter pills |

Both share roving-tabindex + arrow-key nav. Pick by intent, not shape.

## Quick example

```js
import { createRadioGroup } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/radio-group";

const rg = createRadioGroup({
    orientation: "vertical",        // default
    required: true,
    value: "no",                    // initial selection
    onChange: (key, reason) => {},  // fires on selection change
});

rg.attachRoot(groupEl);
rg.attachItem(yesEl, "yes");
rg.attachItem(noEl,  "no");
rg.attachItem(maybeEl, "maybe", { disabled: true });
```

## Options

| Option | Default | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `orientation` | `"vertical"` | `"vertical"` or `"horizontal"`; affects arrow-key direction |
| `value` | `null` | Initial checked key (must match an `attachItem` key) |
| `required` | `false` | Paints `aria-required="true"` on the root |
| `disabled` | `false` | Group-wide disabled state |
| `onChange` | - | `(key, reason) => void`; reasons: `"click"`, `"keyboard"`, `"api"`, `"disabled"`, `"remove"` |

## API

```ts
{
    value(): string | null
    isDisabled(): boolean
    checkedKey: string | null    // sugar for value()
    itemCount: number

    setValue(key: string | null, reason?: string): void
    setDisabled(b: boolean): void
    setItemDisabled(keyOrEl: string | Element, disabled: boolean): void

    attachRoot(el): off
    attachItem(el, key: string, opts?: { disabled?: boolean }): off

    destroy(): void
    destroyed: boolean
}
```

## Keyboard

Per W3C ARIA APG for radio groups:

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Tab | Move focus INTO the group on the checked radio (or first non-disabled if none checked). Does NOT change selection. |
| ArrowDown / ArrowRight | Move focus AND selection to the next radio. Wraps. |
| ArrowUp / ArrowLeft | Move focus AND selection to the previous radio. Wraps. |
| Home | Move to the first non-disabled radio + select it. |
| End | Move to the last non-disabled radio + select it. |
| Space / Enter | Confirm selection on the currently focused radio. (Usually redundant — arrows already select. Included for completeness.) |

Arrow direction follows `orientation`. Horizontal: ArrowLeft/Right.
Vertical: ArrowUp/Down. (Both work in both orientations is non-standard;
we follow the strict APG which means non-orientation arrows are ignored.)

Disabled items are SKIPPED by arrow navigation, Home, and End.
Group-disabled blocks all interaction.

## Painted attributes

### Root (`[data-radio-group-root]`)

- `role="radiogroup"` (only if not pre-set)
- `aria-orientation="horizontal"` (only when explicit; vertical is default)
- `aria-required="true"` (when `required: true`)
- `aria-disabled="true"` (when `setDisabled(true)`)

### Items (`[data-radio-item]`)

- `role="radio"` (only if not pre-set)
- `aria-checked="true"|"false"` (literal string per ARIA spec)
- `aria-disabled="true"` (per-item disabled only)
- `tabindex="0"` on the checked item (or first non-disabled when none checked); `"-1"` on the rest. Managed by roving-focus.
- `data-checked` (boolean — CSS hook; presence === `aria-checked="true"`)
- `data-disabled` (boolean — CSS hook)

## Wrapper (`<lite-radio-group>`)

```html
<lite-radio-group id="bet" value="yes" required>
    <button data-radio-item value="yes">Yes (40%)</button>
    <button data-radio-item value="no">No (60%)</button>
    <button data-radio-item value="abstain" disabled>Abstain</button>
</lite-radio-group>
```

Attributes:
- `value="<key>"` — bidirectional with `host.value`
- `orientation="vertical|horizontal"`
- `required` (boolean)
- `disabled` (boolean — group-wide)

Per-item: each `[data-radio-item]` reads its key from `value="..."` and
checks for a `disabled` attribute.

Host accessors:
- `host.value` / `host.checkedKey`
- `host.isDisabled`, `host.itemCount`
- `host.setValue(key)`, `host.setDisabled(b)`, `host.setItemDisabled(keyOrEl, b)`
- `host._radioGroupInstance` (advanced)

Event: `valuechange` — `{ detail: { value, reason } }`

## Composition

For Twitch Prediction with vote-share meters:

```html
<lite-radio-group id="prediction-outcome">
    <label data-radio-item value="yes" class="outcome">
        <span class="outcome-text">Yes — they'll win</span>
        <lite-meter value="0.42" class="vote-share" aria-label="Yes share">
            <div data-meter-fill></div>
        </lite-meter>
    </label>
    <label data-radio-item value="no" class="outcome">
        <span class="outcome-text">No — they'll lose</span>
        <lite-meter value="0.58" class="vote-share" aria-label="No share">
            <div data-meter-fill></div>
        </lite-meter>
    </label>
</lite-radio-group>
```

The meter is a child element, not a radio item. The radio role is on
the `<label>` (or `<button>`); the meter inside paints its own
`role="meter"` so screen readers announce the share separately.

## CSS contract

Follows `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. `data-checked`/`data-disabled` are
Class-2 booleans. `data-radio-item`/`data-radio-group-root` are Class-4
slot markers.
